questions.text: Which of the following materials is attracted to a magnet?
questions.explanation: Iron is a ferromagnetic material that is strongly attracted to magnets. Wood, plastic, and glass are non-magnetic materials.

questions.text: The north pole of a magnet is attracted to the south pole of another magnet.
questions.explanation: Opposite poles of magnets attract each other (north-south), while like poles repel (north-north or south-south).

questions.text: What happens when you bring two north poles of magnets close to each other?
questions.explanation: Like poles of magnets repel each other. Two north poles or two south poles will push away from each other.

questions.text: Name the device used by pilots and sailors to find direction.
questions.explanation: A magnetic compass uses a freely rotating magnetic needle to indicate direction. The needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field.
